public_goals: Establish a clear accounting of what each party spent on shared credit-card accounts.
Get agreement on a fair split of housing costs during the final shared-living period.
Avoid continuing to carry debt that they believe the other party is primarily responsible for.
private_concerns: Suspects the other party remembers the informal debt agreements differently and may be acting in good faith but with inaccurate recollection.
Fears that without documentation, any proposed split will feel arbitrary and unenforceable.
Worries about long-term credit impact if this drags out unresolved.
red_lines: Will not accept a split based on unverified recollections.
Will not agree to a final allocation before seeing actual statements or documentation.
emotional_triggers: financial decisions being proposed before documentation is established
the other party's version of shared costs being treated as established fact
urgency pressure when the facts are still unclear
disclosure_tendencies: names documentation concerns early and specifically
becomes more flexible once a structured documentation review is agreed as the process
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: becomes more guarded if asked to agree before documentation is in place
opens up once the documentation-first approach is confirmed as the process
starting_positions: Primary position: the credit-card debt should be split based on documented usage, not recollection.
Secondary position: housing costs should be split proportionally to time each party actually occupied the property.
likely_openings: I need us to actually look at the statements before we agree to anything.
I am not saying you spent more than me, I am saying we need to know before we split it.
